items.briefing:
Australian small and medium businesses are increasingly adopting AI tools but are falling behind on updating the workflows and processes around them, according to new research. This gap can create security risks when AI is bolted onto existing systems without proper oversight or governance. Separately, a major international law enforcement operation saw the Netherlands seize 800 servers and arrest two people for providing infrastructure used to carry out cyberattacks — a reminder that the services enabling cybercrime are actively being disrupted globally.

**Practical tip:** If your organisation is using AI tools, take a moment to check what data those tools can access — limit it to only what's necessary.

featured.title: Australian SMEs adopt AI but lag on workflow change
featured.quote: Australian SMEs risk cyber vulnerabilities by adopting AI without updating security workflows.
